title: Earth Brain Event
description: Integrated schema for Earth Brain OS v0.2 Modular Event Architecture. This schema defines the top-level Earth Brain event and references the eight modular layer schemas.

event_id.description: Stable identifier for this Earth Brain OS event.

event_type.description: Machine-readable type of this integrated event.

event_version.description: Version of the integrated Earth Brain event schema or release profile.

updated_at.description: Optional update timestamp for this event.

status.description: Lifecycle status of this integrated event.

description.description: Short human-readable explanation of this integrated event.

layer_order.description: Recommended processing or conceptual order of the modular layers.

cross_layer_links.description: Declared relationships and event pathways between modular layers.

architecture.description: Architecture metadata for the integrated Earth Brain OS event.

examples: Earth Brain OS Modular Event Architecture

schema_profile.description: Schema profile used by this integrated event.

integration_mode.description: How the integrated event composes modular layer schemas.

layer_count.description: Number of modular layers expected in this event.

design_principles.description: Design principles represented by this architecture.

event_context.description: Context metadata for this integrated event.

context_id.description: Stable identifier for the context record.

context_type.description: Type of context represented by this event.

summary.description: Short summary of the event context.

tags.description: Searchable tags for this event context.

layers.description: Container for the eight modular Earth Brain OS layer schemas.

cross_layer_link.description: Relationship and event pathway between two modular layers.

link_id.description: Stable identifier for the cross-layer link.

source_layer.description: Source layer type for this link.

target_layer.description: Target layer type for this link.

relationship.description: Semantic relationship between source and target layers.

description.description: Human-readable description of the cross-layer link.

event_types.description: Event types expected to flow through this cross-layer link.

required.description: Whether this cross-layer link is required for the integrated event profile.

integration_policy.description: Policy for validating and integrating the eight modular layers.

layer_validation_required.description: Whether each layer must validate against its referenced schema.

cross_layer_consistency_required.description: Whether cross-layer relationships must be checked for consistency.

missing_layer_policy.description: Policy applied when one or more required layers are missing.

version_alignment_policy.description: Policy for aligning layer schema versions.

failure_policy.description: Policy applied when integrated validation fails.

future_lifecycle_ref.description: Optional reference to a future lifecycle schema.

trace_requirements.description: Top-level trace requirements for the integrated Earth Brain event.

trace_required.description: Whether the integrated event requires trace records.

layer_trace_required.description: Whether each modular layer must preserve layer-level traces.

cross_layer_trace_required.description: Whether cross-layer event flow must preserve trace continuity.

minimum_provenance_fields.description: Minimum provenance fields required for integrated traceability.

retention_policy.description: Trace retention policy for the integrated event.

governance_hooks.description: Top-level governance hooks for the integrated Earth Brain event.

human_review_boundary.description: Boundary condition that triggers human review.

governance_layer_required.description: Whether Human Governance Layer participation is required.

policy_refs.description: Policy documents or schemas applied to this integrated event.

constitutional_refs.description: Constitutional, principle, or governance reference documents.

escalation_channels.description: Channels or layers used for escalation.
